title: Topological edge states of the hexagonal linear chain
abstract: We study the eigenspectrum properties of a one-dimensional molecular chain composed of hexagonal unit cells. The system features two alternating hopping parameters, resulting in a rich energy spectrum with both dispersive and flat bands. By analyzing the model under periodic and open boundary conditions, we identify two insulating phases separated by a gap-closing transition controlled by the ratio of hopping amplitudes. In the topological phase, realized when the hopping ratio falls below a critical value, edge states emerge that are exponentially localized at the boundaries of finite chains.
summary: This paper explores a 1D hexagonal chain model that generalizes the SSH model, revealing that the complex unit cell leads to both flat bands and topological edge states. It demonstrates that the topological phase transition is sensitive to the system size and that edge states emerge due to interference effects within the hexagonal geometry.
main_problem: The study investigates the emergence of topological edge states and the nature of the phase transition in a one-dimensional molecular chain with a hexagonal unit cell.
main_result: The system exhibits a topological phase characterized by a winding number of 1 and exponentially localized edge states when the hopping ratio is below a critical value, with the transition point being dependent on the chain length.
method: The author uses k-space representation, winding number calculations, and analytical continuation of the wave number into the complex plane to study periodic and open boundary conditions.
model_or_system: A 1D molecular chain composed of hexagonal unit cells (resembling a nanowire of para-connected benzene molecules) described by a single-particle Hückel discrete Hamiltonian with alternating hopping parameters t1 and t2.
key_observables: Energy spectrum (dispersive and flat bands), winding number (topological invariant), and wavefunction localization/decay length.
important_parameters: Hopping ratio r (defined as sqrt(2)t1/t2 or t1/t2 depending on the section), critical ratio rc, and system length N.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes a single-particle Hückel model and considers finite-size effects where the critical transition point depends on N.
figures_summary: Fig 1: Schematic of the hexagonal chain; Fig 2: Visualization of flat-band state localization; Fig 3: Energy band evolution for different r regimes; Fig 4: Schematic of edge state localization; Fig 5: Plots demonstrating the change in real solutions for k; Fig 7: Comparison of bulk vs edge eigenvectors; Fig 8: Graphical representation of the Hamiltonian.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the hexagonal chain model, analyzes the energy spectrum and band structure under periodic boundary conditions, investigates the topological properties via winding numbers, extends the analysis to open boundary conditions to identify edge states, and discusses the size-dependent phase transition.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
